reasoning: The FRED trend analysis projects a downward trajectory for the 3-year Treasury yield, reflecting the broader macroeconomic expectation of Fed rate cuts through 2025. The latest value of 3.76 (as of April 25, the last trading day before the April 27 cutoff) acts as the baseline. The linear model predicts a fall to 3.72 by early May, estimating a 0.40 probability of an increase. While the yield is at a local short-term minimum and could experience mean reversion, the macro downward pressure justifies an initial probability below 0.50. Per the specific instructions, uncertanity increases over longer forecasting horizons, meaning these estimates gradually approach 0.50 for the more distant resolution dates.
